ON THE CLOCK
ISSUE 003
THU JUL 23 2026
The Nashville stuff that’s here for a moment, then gone.
Food, drink, and the cool stuff to do — ranked by how little time you’ve got left to catch it.
This WeekHappening now
Short windows landing this week — here and then gone.
Thu Jul 23 – Sun Jul 26Event
Kindling Arts Festival 2026: UTOPIAS
Various venues

Nashville's experimental arts festival goes full utopian this year — four days of new performance, dance, and interdisciplinary work from some of the city's most fearless makers.

Book it →
Sun Jul 26, 5 PMFood + Drink
Under the Stars: A San Sebastián Wine Dinner
Downtown

A sommelier-guided five-course dinner under the stars at the Fairlane, channeling San Sebastián's Basque coastal cooking — wines poured to match, one Sunday seating, then it's gone.

Book it →
Thursdays at 4 PM through summerFood + Drink
Side B Summer: A Throwback Summer Pop-Up At Sidebar
Sidebar

Sidebar's seasonal throwback pop-up runs Thursdays all summer — a limited-run concept with a defined end built into the name.

Book it →
Wed–Sun, Jul 22–26, 11am–6pmEvent
Kenny Chesney's "American Kids" Bus Pop-Up Experience
Downtown

A free immersive bus pop-up tied to the Chesney exhibit launch — free street-level activation all week at Walk of Fame Park.

Book it →

Blue Material Comedy
Anzie Blue

One-night comedy show at Anzie Blue with tickets from $12 — sales closing soon.

Sat Jul 26, 1 PMEvent
FREE NA Fernet Tasting (Saint Heck) at Both Shops
Killjoy Booze-free Beverage Shop

Killjoy pours Saint Heck's non-alcoholic fernet at both locations — free, no-commitment way to see where the NA spirits scene is actually landing.

Magic Family Brunch
House of Cards

Brunch plus live magic at House of Cards — a niche Sunday morning flex worth knowing about.

Drum Corps International: Marching Music's Major League
Downtown

Elite marching corps competition hits Nashville for one night — a touring spectacle that skips most cities entirely.

Shrek Rave
Germantown

Movies Under the Giant Guitar
Wedgewood-Houston

Mon Jul 27Event
Chris Acker & The Growing Boys at Skinny Dennis
Skinny Dennis

New Orleans-based Chris Acker brings his soulful sound to Skinny Dennis for a one-night stand — the kind of low-key Monday show that punches way above its weight.

Jul 23Music
Sustainable Life Theory Framework East
East Nashville

Nashville-based SLT brings its community sustainability exploration to the east side — part meetup, part think-tank for people who want to reimagine how we live.

Downtown

Oklahoma red-dirt kings at an outdoor Nashville venue — the kind of show where the crowd already knows every word.

The EMO Night Tour
East Nashville

A DJ-driven deep dive into the emo canon — eyeliner optional, knowing every Fall Out Boy B-side is not.

Bingo Loco
Downtown

Touring rave-bingo hybrid with prizes, dance breaks, and chaos — the Irish import that turned dabbers into a party.

It's Giving Global: Salsa Y Mas
East Nashville

Latin dance party in East Nashville — salsa and more in a venue that actually has a good floor for it.

Beyond the Counter Viewing
Downtown

A special viewing event at Woolworth Theatre Friday afternoon — limited details, but the historic venue makes it worth a look.

Edgar Allan Poe Speakeasy: Chapter Two
Franklin

Two-night immersive speakeasy theater mashup — Poe meets prohibition and then it's gone.

Revival at the Ryman
Downtown

A Sunday evening revival show at the Mother Church — one night only, exactly the format the room was built for.

Let's Go Bandanas! Bandana-Making Workshop
Hatch Show Print

Hatch Show Print opens its antique hand-carved printing blocks for an immersive bandana-making session — the kind of tactile, historically rich workshop that books fast.

Seated and finite. When it sells, it’s done.

Friends In Low Places: Christmas In July on the Rooftop
Downtown Nashville

Broadway's Christmas-in-July rooftop party — free entry, seasonal novelty, and the honky-tonk crowd in full holiday-in-summer mode.

Full Moon Pickin' Party
Belle Meade

Monthly full-moon bluegrass gathering in a barn — one night tied to the lunar calendar, then it's gone for another month.

Will Hoge's Eastside Ramble
East Nashville
